title: Andinia mongei
intro: Andinia mongei, also known as Monge's Andinia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for wound healing, reducing fever, and preventing infections. Bioactive constituents of Andinia mongei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Andinia mongei is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Andinia mongei is a species of orchid in the subtribe Andiniinae, which is part of the tribe Epidendreae. This subtribe is a member of the orchid family, Orchidaceae. The Andiniinae subtribe is characterized by its unique flowers and growth habits. Within the subtribe, Andinia mongei is classified as a distinct species due to its unique characteristics. Further classification within the species is not well-documented.
